name: Elgato HD60 X External Capture Card — 4K30 / 1080p60 HDR10+

specs.Interface: USB-C external (works with PC and Mac)
specs.Passthrough: 4K60 HDR10+ passthrough to TV via HDMI
specs.Console Compatibility: PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, PC
specs.Mac Support: Compatible with macOS Big Sur and later

whatWorks: The HD60 X's external USB-C design makes it the most accessible capture card for beginners and anyone with a single PC or laptop setup. There are no PCIe slots required, no desktop-only restriction, and no driver installation beyond plugging in the USB-C cable. On modern Macs and Windows laptops, the card is detected and functional in under two minutes — plug and play in the most literal sense.

4K60 HDR10+ passthrough is the headline feature — your PS5 or Xbox Series X still outputs full-quality 4K60 HDR to your TV, while the HD60 X captures a 4K30 or 1080p60 stream for recording or broadcasting. The passthrough quality is transparent, meaning gaming is completely unaffected by the capture process for day-to-day play.

Compatibility breadth is unmatched in this price range. PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, and PC all work without any configuration adjustments. The card automatically handles HDCP-free content and works across streaming platforms via OBS without requiring a special plugin or driver package beyond standard USB device installation.

The USB-C form factor means the card itself is smaller than a deck of cards, making it genuinely portable. Console streamers who travel, play at friends' setups, or run gaming events can pack the HD60 X in a bag with their laptop and have a complete streaming rig without additional hardware. This portability is a real differentiator over internal capture cards.
worthKnowing: Capture is limited to 4K30 or 1080p60 — not both simultaneously in 4K60 mode. Most streamers and YouTube creators work at 1080p60 for their output, so this limitation is rarely a problem in practice, but buyers expecting 4K60 capture (not just passthrough) should be aware. The Elgato 4K60 Pro MK.2 (internal PCIe) is the product that captures at 4K60.

The USB-C connection requires USB 3.0 bandwidth to function correctly. While USB-C is the physical connector, the cable and port must be USB 3.0 or higher — some older USB-C ports on thin laptops only provide USB 2.0 bandwidth, which is insufficient for 4K30 capture. Check your laptop's USB-C specification before assuming compatibility.

HDCP-protected content from streaming apps like Netflix, Disney+, and Spotify cannot be captured — this is a legal content protection requirement, not an Elgato limitation. Game capture from PS5, Xbox, and Switch works without restriction, but any HDCP-flagged source will result in a black capture screen by design.

The 4K Capture Utility software provides basic recording and monitoring but is not a full streaming solution. For live streaming with alerts, scenes, overlays, and chat integration, you need to pair it with OBS or Streamlabs. The good news is that OBS integration requires zero configuration — the HD60 X appears as a standard video capture device.

commonComplaints.title: No 4K60 capture — passthrough only
commonComplaints.body: The most common disappointment is discovering that 4K60 is passthrough-only — actual capture is limited to 4K30 or 1080p60. Buyers who read the marketing without checking the technical specs expected to record and stream at 4K60 simultaneously and were surprised by the limitation.

commonComplaints.title: USB 3.0 requirement catches some users
commonComplaints.body: Several laptop users found their USB-C port was USB 2.0 speed, causing choppy or failed capture. The box specifies USB 3.0 required but the physical connector looks identical, leading to confusion when setup fails on older thin-and-light laptops.

commonComplaints.title: HDCP blocks streaming app capture
commonComplaints.body: Users who wanted to capture Netflix, YouTube, or other streaming content discovered HDCP blocking prevents this entirely. This is a legal requirement and expected behavior, but the number of negative reviews on this topic suggests the limitation is not clearly communicated at point of sale.

commonComplaints.title: Cable included is short
commonComplaints.body: The included USB-C cable is short and some users found it did not reach comfortably between their console and PC position. A longer USB-C 3.0 cable resolves the issue but is an additional purchase for setups where the PC is across the desk from the console.

verdict: The Elgato HD60 X is the best external capture card for console and laptop streaming setups, combining plug-and-play simplicity, broad PS5/Xbox/Switch compatibility, and HDR10+ passthrough in a genuinely portable package. The 4K30 capture ceiling and HDCP content restrictions are real limitations that buyers should understand upfront, but for the core use case — a beginner or mobile streamer capturing console gameplay at 1080p60 — it delivers a flawless experience at a reasonable price.

realBuyers.quote: Set it up in ten minutes. No drivers, no complicated configuration — plugged into my laptop, opened OBS, and the PS5 capture was just there. Exactly the plug-and-play experience I wanted.
The 4K60 passthrough means my TV gaming experience is completely unchanged. I would not even know the card is in the chain if I had not put it there myself. Really impressive for the price.
I bring this to LAN events and gaming nights. The whole streaming setup fits in my laptop bag. No one else at these events can capture and stream as easily as I can.
Works perfectly on my MacBook Pro M3. I was worried about Mac compatibility but it was detected immediately and OBS saw it without any issues. Great for console streaming from a Mac.

sources.title: Elgato HD60 X Review — The Verge
Elgato HD60 X Review — Digital Trends

metaDescription: Elgato HD60 X review: The best external capture card for PS5, Xbox, and Switch streaming with 4K60 HDR passthrough. $159 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Elgato HD60 X External Capture Card (ASIN B09NMH2QCN) is available on Amazon.ca for $159 CAD and is the definitive plug-and-play capture solution for console streamers, supporting PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Nintendo Switch, and PC via a single USB-C connection with no PCIe slot or desktop required. The 4K60 HDR10+ HDMI passthrough keeps your TV gaming experience completely unaffected.
